originalAlarm.description: The value written to `&codec_hw_write` is never used.

presenceCondition:
(and (= CONFIG_SND_FSI_DA7210 true)
     (= CONFIG_SND_FSI_AK4642 true)
     (= CONFIG_SND_SOC_AK4642 false)
     (= CONFIG_SND_SOC_DA7210 false)
     (= CONFIG_I2C false))
model:
(define-fun CONFIG_SND_FSI_AK4642 () Bool
  true)
(define-fun CONFIG_SND_SOC_AK4642 () Bool
  false)
(define-fun CONFIG_SND_SOC_DA7210 () Bool
  false)
(define-fun CONFIG_I2C () Bool
  false)
(define-fun CONFIG_SND_FSI_DA7210 () Bool
  true)
reasoning: The PC is infeasible due to derived-macro contradictions in 66517915e09.c: FSI_AK4642=T causes '#define CONFIG_SND_SOC_AK4642' at L2, yet PC requires SOC_AK4642=F; FSI_DA7210=T causes '#define CONFIG_SND_SOC_DA7210' at L6, yet PC requires SOC_DA7210=F. Both constraints are contradictory — this configuration cannot compile coherently. Additionally I2C=F means ak4642_init() (where L24 lives) is under #ifdef SOC_AK4642 which would be defined by FSI_AK4642=T, but SOC_AK4642=F prevents that. Classification: FALSE.
